---
title: "6-22-2026-BulkRNA_PCA_Anlaysis"
output: html_document
---
```{rsetup }


## ============================================================
## pca_suite.R
## Full set of QC PCAs for the OPA1 / ATF4 / AO bulk RNA-seq set.
##
##   (1) All samples            color = genotype, shape = diet      [1 PCA]
##   (2) Per line (OPA1/ATF4/AO) color = diet,     shape = WT/KO     [3 PCAs]
##   (3) Per diet (CD / HFD)     color = genotype                    [2 PCAs]
##   (4) Per genotype group      color = diet                        [6 PCAs]
##
## VST is computed ONCE on the full matrix (blind); each subset then
## re-selects its own top-variable genes before prcomp.
## ============================================================

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(DESeq2)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(ggrepel)
  library(matrixStats)
  library(patchwork)
 library(pheatmap)      
})

tsv     <- "~/Desktop/Lab_Streeter_new/BULKRNASEQ_AJ/DATA/salmon.merged.gene_counts_length_scaled.relabeled.tsv"
outdir   <- "~/Desktop/Lab_Streeter_new/BULKRNASEQ_AJ/RESULTS/PCA_ANALYSIS"
 # writes PNGs to working dir
ntop_all <- 2000         # genes for full / per-diet PCAs
ntop_sub <- 1000         # genes for per-line / per-group PCAs

## ---- 1. Load + metadata (ORIGINAL labels from column names) -------------
raw <- read.delim(tsv, check.names = FALSE)
rownames(raw) <- make.unique(raw$gene_name)
mat <- as.matrix(raw[, !(colnames(raw) %in% c("gene_id", "gene_name"))])

parts <- strsplit(colnames(mat), "_")
meta <- data.frame(
  row.names  = colnames(mat),
  genotype   = vapply(parts, function(x) paste(x[1], x[2], sep = "_"), ""),
  geno_line  = vapply(parts, `[`, "", 1),   # OPA1 / ATF4 / AO
  geno_short = vapply(parts, `[`, "", 2),   # WT / KO
  diet       = vapply(parts, `[`, "", 3),
  id         = vapply(parts, `[`, "", 4)
)
geno_lv <- c("OPA1_WT","OPA1_KO","ATF4_WT","ATF4_KO","AO_WT","AO_KO")
meta$genotype   <- factor(meta$genotype, levels = geno_lv)
meta$geno_short <- factor(meta$geno_short, levels = c("WT","KO"))
meta$diet       <- factor(meta$diet, levels = c("CD","HFD"))

## ---- OPTIONAL: use CORRECTED labels instead -----------------------------
## cd <- read.csv("colData_corrected.csv", row.names = 1)
## cd <- cd[cd$status != "remove", ]          # drop excluded partial responders
## # for PENDING_PCA samples keep their original group so you can SEE them:
## cd$grp <- ifelse(cd$final_genotype == "PENDING_PCA",
##                  cd$orig_genotype, cd$final_genotype)
## mat  <- mat[, rownames(cd)]
## meta <- meta[rownames(cd), ]; meta$genotype <- factor(cd$grp, levels = geno_lv)

## ---- 2. DESeq2 VST (once, blind) ----------------------------------------
dds <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(round(mat), meta, design = ~ 1)
dds <- dds[rowSums(counts(dds) >= 10) >= 3, ]
vsd <- vst(dds, blind = TRUE)

## ---- 3. Palettes --------------------------------------------------------
geno_cols  <- c(OPA1_WT="#c0392b", OPA1_KO="#e8836a",
                ATF4_WT="#1f4e9c", ATF4_KO="#6fa8dc",
                AO_WT  ="#1e5631", AO_KO  ="#93c47d")
diet_cols  <- c(CD = "#4C72B0", HFD = "#DD8452")
short_cols <- c(WT = "#2c7fb8", KO = "#de2d26")
diet_shp   <- c(CD = 16, HFD = 17)
short_shp  <- c(WT = 16, KO = 17)

## ---- 4. PCA helper ------------------------------------------------------
run_pca <- function(samples, ntop, color_by, shape_by = NULL, title) {
  m   <- assay(vsd)[, samples, drop = FALSE]
  rv  <- rowVars(m)
  sel <- order(rv, decreasing = TRUE)[seq_len(min(ntop, sum(rv > 0)))]
  pc  <- prcomp(t(m[sel, ]), center = TRUE, scale. = FALSE)
  ve  <- round(100 * pc$sdev^2 / sum(pc$sdev^2), 1)
  d   <- data.frame(pc$x[, 1:2], meta[samples, ])

  pt_aes <- aes(color = .data[[color_by]])
  if (!is.null(shape_by)) pt_aes <- modifyList(pt_aes, aes(shape = .data[[shape_by]]))

  g <- ggplot(d, aes(PC1, PC2)) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "grey85", linewidth = .3) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = "grey85", linewidth = .3) +
    geom_point(pt_aes, size = 3.4, stroke = .4) +
    geom_text_repel(aes(label = id), size = 2.5, color = "grey25",
                    max.overlaps = Inf, segment.size = .2,
                    min.segment.length = .3, seed = 1) +
    labs(x = sprintf("PC1 (%.1f%%)", ve[1]),
         y = sprintf("PC2 (%.1f%%)", ve[2]), title = title) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
    theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
          plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11))

  col_pal <- switch(color_by, genotype = geno_cols, diet = diet_cols,
                    geno_short = short_cols)
  g <- g + scale_color_manual(values = col_pal)
  if (!is.null(shape_by)) {
    shp <- switch(shape_by, diet = diet_shp, geno_short = short_shp)
    g <- g + scale_shape_manual(values = shp)
  }
  g
}

S <- function(...) rownames(meta)[with(meta, ...)]   # subset-by-condition helper

## ---- (1) All samples ----------------------------------------------------
p_all <- run_pca(rownames(meta), ntop_all, "genotype", "diet",
                 "All samples")
ggsave(file.path(outdir, "pca_1_all_samples.png"), p_all,
       width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 150)

## ---- (2) Per line: OPA1 / ATF4 / AO  (color diet, shape WT/KO) -----------
p_opa1 <- run_pca(S(geno_line == "OPA1"), ntop_sub, "diet", "geno_short", "OPA1 line")
p_atf4 <- run_pca(S(geno_line == "ATF4"), ntop_sub, "diet", "geno_short", "ATF4 line")
p_ao   <- run_pca(S(geno_line == "AO"),   ntop_sub, "diet", "geno_short", "AO line")
ggsave(file.path(outdir, "pca_2_per_line.png"),
       p_opa1 + p_atf4 + p_ao + plot_layout(nrow = 1),
       width = 16, height = 5.5, dpi = 150)

## ---- (3) Per diet: CD / HFD  (color genotype) ---------------------------
p_cd  <- run_pca(S(diet == "CD"),  ntop_all, "genotype", NULL, "CD samples")
p_hfd <- run_pca(S(diet == "HFD"), ntop_all, "genotype", NULL, "HFD samples")
ggsave(file.path(outdir, "pca_3_per_diet.png"),
       p_cd + p_hfd + plot_layout(nrow = 1),
       width = 14, height = 6, dpi = 150)

## ---- (4) Per genotype group  (color diet) -------------------------------
group_plots <- lapply(geno_lv, function(grp) {
  samples_grp <- rownames(meta)[meta$genotype == grp]
  run_pca(samples_grp, ntop_sub, "diet", NULL, grp)
})


ggsave(file.path(outdir, "pca_4_per_group.png"),
       wrap_plots(group_plots, ncol = 3) +
         plot_annotation(title = "PCA per genotype group (color = diet)"),
       width = 16, height = 9, dpi = 150)

cat("Wrote:\n  pca_1_all_samples.png\n  pca_2_per_line.png",
    "\n  pca_3_per_diet.png\n  pca_4_per_group.png\n")


## ============================================================
## (5) Heatmap of top PC-driver genes (top 20 up / 20 down loadings)
##     "genes used in the PCA" = highest & lowest loadings on a PC,
##     drawn from the same top-variable-gene set the PCA was built on.
## ============================================================

pc_driver_heatmap <- function(samples, ntop, pc = 1, n = 20,
                              file, title, cap = 3) {
  ## same gene selection + PCA as run_pca()
  m   <- assay(vsd)[, samples, drop = FALSE]
  rv  <- rowVars(m)
  sel <- order(rv, decreasing = TRUE)[seq_len(min(ntop, sum(rv > 0)))]
  pcobj <- prcomp(t(m[sel, ]), center = TRUE, scale. = FALSE)

  ## top n positive- and negative-loading genes on the chosen PC
  load  <- pcobj$rotation[, pc]
  up    <- names(sort(load, decreasing = TRUE))[seq_len(n)]   # + loading
  down  <- names(sort(load, decreasing = FALSE))[seq_len(n)]  # - loading
  genes <- c(up, down)

  ## z-score each gene across these samples, cap extremes
  z <- t(scale(t(m[genes, ])))
  z[is.na(z)] <- 0
  z[z >  cap] <-  cap
  z[z < -cap] <- -cap

  ## order columns by PC score so the gradient is visible
  z <- z[, order(pcobj$x[, pc])]

  ## annotations
  ann <- meta[colnames(z), c("genotype", "diet")]
  rann <- data.frame(PC_loading = rep(c("up (+)", "down (-)"), each = n),
                     row.names = genes)
  ann_colors <- list(genotype = geno_cols, diet = diet_cols,
                     PC_loading = c("up (+)" = "#B2182B", "down (-)" = "#2166AC"))

  breaks <- seq(-cap, cap, length.out = 101)
  pal    <- colorRampPalette(c("#2166AC", "white", "#B2182B"))(100)

  pheatmap(z, color = pal, breaks = breaks,
           cluster_rows = TRUE, cluster_cols = FALSE,
           annotation_col = ann, annotation_row = rann,
           annotation_colors = ann_colors,
           show_colnames = TRUE, fontsize_row = 7, fontsize_col = 7,
           main = title, filename = file, width = 10, height = 9.5)
}

## Heatmap for the all-samples PCA, PC1 drivers (top 20 up / 20 down)
pc_driver_heatmap(rownames(meta), ntop_all, pc = 1, n = 20,
                  file  = file.path(outdir, "pca_5_PC1_top20_heatmap.png"),
                  title = "Top 20 +/- PC1 loading genes (all samples)")

cat("  pca_5_PC1_top20_heatmap.png\n")

## ============================================================
## (6) Heatmaps for EVERY PCA  (top 20 + bottom 20 PC1 loadings)
##     One heatmap per PCA, same samples + same ntop as that PCA.
## ============================================================

## subset vectors computed directly (avoids the with()/lapply scoping issue)
sub_of <- function(cond) rownames(meta)[cond]

hm_specs <- c(
  ## (1) all samples
  list(list(s = rownames(meta),                     ntop = ntop_all,
            tag = "all_samples",  ttl = "All samples")),
  ## (2) per line
  list(list(s = sub_of(meta$geno_line == "OPA1"),   ntop = ntop_sub,
            tag = "line_OPA1",    ttl = "OPA1 line")),
  list(list(s = sub_of(meta$geno_line == "ATF4"),   ntop = ntop_sub,
            tag = "line_ATF4",    ttl = "ATF4 line")),
  list(list(s = sub_of(meta$geno_line == "AO"),     ntop = ntop_sub,
            tag = "line_AO",      ttl = "AO line")),
  ## (3) per diet
  list(list(s = sub_of(meta$diet == "CD"),          ntop = ntop_all,
            tag = "diet_CD",      ttl = "CD samples")),
  list(list(s = sub_of(meta$diet == "HFD"),         ntop = ntop_all,
            tag = "diet_HFD",     ttl = "HFD samples"))
)

## (4) per genotype group (6)
for (g in geno_lv)
  hm_specs <- c(hm_specs,
    list(list(s = sub_of(meta$genotype == g),       ntop = ntop_sub,
              tag = paste0("group_", g),  ttl = g)))

## draw them all
for (sp in hm_specs) {
  pc_driver_heatmap(
    samples = sp$s, ntop = sp$ntop, pc = 1, n = 20,
    file  = file.path(outdir, paste0("pca_hm_", sp$tag, "_PC1_top20.png")),
    title = paste0(sp$ttl, " - top20 +/- PC1 loadings"))
  cat("  pca_hm_", sp$tag, "_PC1_top20.png\n", sep = "")
}




```
